stories.title: Root-cause and re-enable per-package RPM signing
stories.review_note: CI-runtime verification pending: rpmsign --addsign + rpm --checksig -v cannot be empirically confirmed until a v0.2.1 tag is pushed. Research recipe + agent warm-up + Ubuntu %__gpg override all in place; fallback ladder (--define CLI, or re-disable + v0.2.2 hotfix) documented inline at release.yml:382-390.

stories.title: CI regression guard for reprepro arch idempotency
stories.review_note: Implementation uses stricter awk field-exact match ($3 == path) over the PRD AC2 regex — correct: avoids false positives on comment lines or .gz variants. Guard validated locally with 3 synthetic InRelease scenarios (regression/healthy/missing-entry) via curl-override harness; runtime CI verification will land on the next v0.2.1+ publish.

stories.title: Auto-chain repo-publish.yml via workflow_run
stories.review_note: if: expression is a superset of PRD AC2 — adds (a) workflow_dispatch passthrough (required by AC4) and (b) prerelease filter (preserves v0.2.0 stable-repo-only semantics; mirrors release.yml:903). Sanity-check first step logs parent event metadata and fails fast on empty/non-semver TAG — closes PRD Risk #10 (head_branch community-split) via observable + recoverable fast-fail. CI-runtime verification pending: chain fires on the next v0.2.2+ tag push. PRD typo: AC1 at L205 reads workflows: ["release"] lowercase; correct value is "Release" (matches release.yml:1).

stories.title: Post-publish end-to-end smoke test in CI
stories.review_note: Two new jobs (smoke-post-publish-ubuntu, smoke-post-publish-fedora) appended to repo-publish.yml with needs: publish + success guard. Uses GHA container: directive instead of PRD-literal 'podman run' — matches project convention (existing release.yml smoke-test-* jobs) and abstracts the runtime. Version assertion derives 'paneflow X.Y.Z' dynamically from ${TAG#v}%%-* so works for stable and operator-dispatched prerelease publishes. Keyring path aligned to /usr/share/keyrings/paneflow-archive.gpg matching packaging/debian/postinst:21 (review catch). Fedora heredoc YAML-block-scalar-strip verified empirically: [paneflow] lands at column 0, dnf parses correctly. CI-runtime verification pending first v0.2.2+ publish.

stories.title: Document gh secret set stdin-redirect quirk
stories.review_note: Added `### Setting GPG secrets on GitHub (US-005)` subsection to docs/release-signing.md §2 with DO/DON'T pipe-vs-stdin-redirect contrast, canonical 4-step incantation (gpg export → chmod 600 → gh secret set < file → shred -u), and v0.2.0 retry #5 historical note with the exact grep-matchable error string. Section 6 truncation bullet cross-references the new subsection and also inlines the error string for double grep coverage.

stories.title: cargo-deny integration with warn-only posture
stories.review_note: New deny.toml at repo root + security-audit job in ci.yml. Fail-forward posture via continue-on-error: true at JOB level (broader than PRD AC3's step-level; intentional: a broken install step also won't block CI during v0.2.1 warn-only period). allow-org list verified against actual Cargo.lock: {zed-industries, smol-rs, proptest-rs} — exact match. Per Phase 5 policy no local cargo-deny run; first CI run on next Rust-affecting PR/push is the definitive AC3 check. v0.2.2 follow-ups: (a) switch cargo install to taiki-e/install-action or EmbarkStudios/cargo-deny-action for ~90s CI savings + better supply-chain posture, (b) flip continue-on-error to false + elevate license severity to deny once lockfile is audit-clean.

stories.title: Fix Windows Explorer /select,<path> arg concat + sidebar comment
stories.review_note: workspace_ops/mod.rs:688-691 rewritten: OsString::from("/select,") + push(path.as_os_str()) → single argv token. sidebar/mod.rs:504-518 comment corrected: open::that uses ShellExecuteW (Win32 API), functionally equivalent to cmd /C start for https:// URLs but NOT the same mechanism. Local gates: cargo fmt/clippy (Linux) + cargo test (41 passed). cargo check --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc: Rust source clean; native C/asm deps (psm/stacker/ring) fail on Linux host due to missing MSVC — CI windows-check job provides native validation. Closes v0.2.0 US-011 SHOULD_FIX + CONSIDER review_note (US-010 will propagate status). AC5 (Windows 11 hardware smoke) operator-gated — story remains IN_REVIEW pending hardware validation.

stories.title: Resolve Intel Mac matrix entry status
stories.review_note: REMOVED path chosen per PRD Technical Considerations + Risk #6 recommendation. Commented-out x86_64-apple-darwin block fully deleted from release.yml (not just re-commented); replaced with dated CLOSED TARGET rationale citing both blockers (Apple Dev secrets + macos-13 queue SLA) and cross-referencing memory/project_macos_signing.md + runbook §Supported release targets + tasks/prd-macos-port.md. aarch64-apple-darwin leg kept in-matrix (continue-on-error: true) with expanded comment explaining BOTH-macOS-legs-deferred posture for AC6 consistency; rationale for keeping Apple Silicon but removing Intel: macos-14 queue is fast (~10 min) and leg catches Rust cross-compile regressions even without signing, while macos-13 queue blocks downstream needs:. New docs/release-runbook.md §Supported release targets section lists all 6 targets with active/best-effort/closed status, gate levels, and per-target restore requirements. v0.3.0 commitment paragraph binds restoration of both macOS legs to the first signed macOS release cut. Reviewer caught copy-paste error in Windows ARM64 row (was x86_64-pc-windows-msvc, now aarch64-pc-windows-msvc) — fixed. actionlint exit 0. RESTORED path explicitly deferred to v0.3.0.

stories.title: Upgrade GitHub Actions away from Node.js 20
stories.review_note: actions/checkout@v4 → @v5 (11 occurrences across 5 workflow files); dorny/paths-filter@v3 → @v4 (ci.yml:31); ilammy/msvc-dev-cmd@v1 retained (no Node 24 tag upstream as of 2026-04-21, issue #99 + PR #101 open) with FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: "true" scoped to the windows-check job env + explicit removal-condition comment. v0.2.2 follow-ups: (a) verify nathanvaughn/actions-cloudflare-purge@1856dd6f... (SHA-pinned) declares node24; (b) pre-existing SC2016 actionlint warning at update-cask.yml:148 is unrelated to Node migration and out-of-scope per Scope Creep Guard. AC5 (zero Node 20 deprecation warnings) verifiable on next CI run. AC6 actionlint passes on all 4 files I touched.

stories.title: Close residual v0.2.0 review notes
stories.review_note: 4 review_notes closed in tasks/prd-v0.2.0-release-hardening-status.json: US-003 (cf-cache-status → addressed by v0.2.1 US-002 + US-004), US-009 (cp -R → ditto deferred to v0.3.0), US-010 (msiexec 1618 → WIN-001 in v0.3.0), US-011 (explorer two-argv + sidebar comment → fixed by v0.2.1 US-007). Scope note: US-011 was not in the PRD US-010 AC list but is covered by EP-003 DoD (no review_note left unclosed with rationale). All 3 original CONSIDER keywords preserved as grep breadcrumbs in the closure text. jq validates cleanly; v0.2.0 PRD status remains DONE.
